message:
test(goldens): make limit_prefix_ordering golden reproducible across temp-dir runs

The golden harness in tests/golden_metamorphic_search.rs was
writing `source_path` values as the full absolute path
(`/tmp/.tmpXYZ/.../amp/session_2.jsonl`), which meant every run
produced a byte-different golden because the temp dir random
suffix changed. That defeats the point of a golden test —
subsequent runs would either fail or silently rewrite the pinned
file with new tmp paths.

Two coupled fixes, committed together because the golden regen
depends on the harness change:

tests/golden_metamorphic_search.rs

- Inside `golden_limit_prefix_ordering`, capture the tempdir
  root as `dir_prefix` and define a `strip` closure that removes
  it from the head of any hit path and trims the leading `/` so
  the surviving path is relative to the tempdir root
  (e.g. `amp/session_2.jsonl`).
- Rewrite the `extract` closure so the per-hit entry tuple is
  (relative_path, agent, line_number, json_value), collect
  those into `entries`, and sort by (relative_path,
  line_number). Tantivy doesn't guarantee a stable hit order
  for ties at equal score, and the non-deterministic temp path
  was hiding that — with the path normalized, the tie-breaker
  shows up, so we sort explicitly to pin it.
- Emit the sorted `json_value`s into the `limit_5_hits` /
  `limit_10_hits` / `limit_15_hits` arrays. This is what the
  golden compares against.

tests/golden/metamorphic/limit_prefix_ordering.json

- Regenerate the entire snapshot so `source_path` strings are
  all relative (`amp/session_0.jsonl`, not
  `/tmp/.tmpMT1QdH/amp/session_0.jsonl`) and the hit order
  matches the new (path, line_number) sort. Total file shape is
  unchanged — same three limit arrays, same count per limit,
  same per-hit shape — just stable now.

After this, `cargo test --test golden_metamorphic_search` is
reproducible across machines and runs; without it, the golden
round-trip would have been flaky from the first day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
